questionHtml:
The equilibrium 2H2 (g) + N2 (g) ⇌ N2H4 (g) has an equilibrium constant, K, at 150 °C. 

What is the equilibrium constant at 150 °C, for the reverse reaction?

N2H4 (g) ⇌ 2H2 (g) + N2 (g)


A.  K

B.  K−1

C.  −K

D.  2K

meta.title: The equilibrium 2H2 (g) + N2 (g) ⇌ N2H4 (g) has an equilibrium constant, K, at 150 °C. What is the equilibrium constant at 150 °C, for the reverse reaction? N2H4 (g) ⇌ 2H2 (g) + N2
